label: Localized ant bites without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit a local skin reaction from ant bites.
found: Your clinician diagnosed localized ant bites. We did not find signs of anaphylaxis, serious allergic reaction, spreading infection, or a bite emergency today.
home: Wash the area gently with soap and water.
Use cool compresses for itching or swelling.
meds: Use itch or allergy medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
return: Trouble breathing, throat swelling, vomiting after a bite, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Spreading redness, pus, fever, red streaks, or worsening warmth.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized ant bites.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider bite, tick-borne illness concern, or snakebite concern.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider or scorpion concern, tick-borne illness concern, snakebite concern, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus insect bite material supports local bite care, itch control, and escalation for allergic reactions, infection, or systemic symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized ant bites with anaphylaxis and infection red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized ant bites

label: Localized plant contact dermatitis without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit localized contact dermatitis from plant exposure.
found: Your clinician diagnosed localized plant contact dermatitis. We did not find signs of anaphylaxis, severe skin reaction, eye or genital involvement, or spreading infection today.
home: Wash the area gently with soap and water.
Wash clothing or objects that may still have plant oil or irritant on them.
meds: Use skin or itch medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover steroid or antibiotic creams unless your clinician said they are safe.
return: Trouble breathing, throat swelling, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Fever, spreading redness, pus, red streaks, or worsening warmth.
Rash near the eyes or genitals, skin peeling, mouth sores, or symptoms that worsen.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized plant contact dermatitis.
No anaphylaxis, infection, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye/genital location, burn or chemical wound, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or immunocompromised host.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, infection concern, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye or genital rash location, burn or chemical wound, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus contact dermatitis material supports irritant/allergic rash framing, trigger avoidance, washing exposed skin and objects, itch care, and escalation for infection, severe reaction, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to localized plant contact dermatitis without systemic allergic reaction, infection, eye/genital involvement, or severe cutaneous reaction.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized plant contact dermatitis

label: Localized soap irritant contact dermatitis without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit localized irritant contact dermatitis from soap exposure.
found: Your clinician diagnosed localized soap irritant contact dermatitis. We did not find signs of anaphylaxis, severe skin reaction, eye or genital involvement, or spreading infection today.
home: Wash the area gently with water if the soap is still on the skin.
Avoid the triggering soap or irritant.
meds: Use skin or itch medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover steroid or antibiotic creams unless your clinician said they are safe.
return: Trouble breathing, throat swelling, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Fever, spreading redness, pus, red streaks, or worsening warmth.
Rash near the eyes or genitals, skin peeling, mouth sores, or symptoms that worsen.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized soap irritant contact dermatitis.
No anaphylaxis, infection, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye/genital location, burn or chemical wound, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or immunocompromised host.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, infection concern, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, eye or genital rash location, burn or chemical wound, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized soap irritant rash
